description: Natural-language query — top 10 videos by watch time generates valid DuckDB SQL and returns results

input.query_description: What are my top 10 videos by watch time this quarter?

expected.columns: non-empty array including a watch-time column
expected.rows: non-empty array when DuckDB connected (up to 10 rows)
expected.schema: non-null object with column names and types

prohibit: sql_executed containing INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE, or DROP
rows with fabricated data not from DuckDB execution
schema = null when DuckDB successfully inferred it

description: DuckDB not connected — guidance-only output with SQL and empty rows

expected.sql_executed: non-null string containing valid DuckDB SQL with read_csv_auto
expected.retrieval_gaps: contains note about DuckDB MCP not connected

prohibit: rows with any data when DuckDB is not connected
fabricated result rows populated as examples

description: User-supplied SQL with DROP TABLE rejected — read-only enforcement

input.sql: DROP TABLE IF EXISTS old_data; SELECT * FROM read_csv_auto('youtube_export.csv')

expected.retrieval_gaps: contains rejection note about write operation (DROP) in user SQL
expected.sql_executed: does not contain DROP — rejected portion stripped or entire query refused

prohibit: sql_executed containing DROP, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE, or CREATE
no error or warning about the write operation in user SQL
